subject: Cloud Computing
title: AWS and the Startup World
description: A quick win: understand AWS and the Startup World.

hook.text: AWS gives startups up to $100,000 in free credits. It's an investment — they're betting you'll grow.

buildup.text: Programs like AWS Activate helped launch thousands of startups that couldn't afford servers.

discovery.text: Airbnb, Slack, and Stripe all started on AWS credits. Free cloud was their launchpad.

twist.text: By the time credits expire, the startup is dependent on AWS. Migrating would cost more than staying.

climax.text: The strategy works brilliantly. Today's credit-fueled startup is tomorrow's million-dollar account.

punchline.text: Free credits today. Loyal customer for a decade.

quiz.question: Why does AWS offer free credits to startups?
options: Startups that grow on AWS become dependent long-term paying customers
AWS is required by law to support small businesses
Credits are only given to companies AWS plans to acquire
